# opencode-unison

> OpenCode plugin that gives your coding agent persistent memory via the Unison brain —
> a hosted, path-addressable knowledge store any agent can read and write over one HTTP API.

**Agents: start with [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md)** — it gets you from install to a working
memory-enabled OpenCode session in five minutes, then shows how to contribute.

The plugin hooks into OpenCode's `chat.message` and `event` lifecycle. On every session
start it injects relevant project and user memories; on every assistant reply it detects
memory-trigger phrases and nudges the agent to save; on compaction it preserves context
across summarization. Auth is a `usk_...` bearer key — see `AGENTS.md` for how to get one.

## Docs
- [README](README.md): install, quickstart, env vars, configuration reference
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): how an agent installs, authenticates, and uses this plugin
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md): build commands, conventions, PR checklist
- [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md): vulnerability reporting + credential handling policy

## Usage
- Install: `bunx opencode-unison@latest install`
- Authenticate: `bunx opencode-unison@latest login` (browser) or `--headless` (OTP)
- Set key directly: `export UNISON_TOKEN=usk_live_...`
- Config file: `~/.config/opencode/unison.jsonc`

## Examples
- Write a memory: `unison(mode: "add", content: "...", scope: "project")`
- Search memories: `unison(mode: "search", query: "...", scope: "project")`
- List memories: `unison(mode: "list", scope: "user")`
- Check status: `unison(mode: "status")`
